preamble: Fortition of the lateral approximant [l] to a lateral fricative [ɬ] is a rarer shift, often found in specific phonological environments or as part of consonant mutations.

examples.note: Historical developments in Northwest Caucasian languages show shifts between lateral approximants and lateral fricatives.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
